articles.title: Elder Henry D. Moyle—The New Apostle (Continuation)
articles.author: Elder Harold B. Lee
articles.complete_text: dividual effort expended—nothing is impossible." In a just cause which most men have thought hopeless and have quit fighting, Henry D. Moyle has just begun the fight. A man with such fighting qualities is certain to have made some people disaffected toward him, but his friends, the friends of righteousness, are legion and, as the Church members come to know him better, they will know him as an able preacher of righteousness, a fearless defender of the faith, and a loyal and powerful advocate of the truth. Such is, in part, the man Henry D. Moyle, whom the Council of the Twelve unanimously accepts into their quorum, and the Church welcomes as one of its leaders.
articles.notes: This is clearly the conclusion of the Elder Henry D. Moyle article that was continued from page 303, as indicated by the header text

misc_summary: Contains various advertisements for businesses including Deseret News Press, Sears, L.D.S. Business College, Bookcraft, Brigham Young University summer programs, religious books and materials, Daynes Music Co., Beneficial Life Insurance, and other commercial content typical of magazine advertising sections.
